budget_wines.name: Royal Tokaji Late Harvest
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Royal Tokaji cellar door at Mád; widely distributed in UK via Berry Bros, in Hungary through specialist retailers and the producer's own Budapest outlet.
budget_wines.description: Royal Tokaji's entry-level Late Harvest is the gateway to the producer's portfolio, made from Furmint and Hárslevelü with residual sweetness from naturally late-harvested grapes. Fresh apricot and citrus peel on a lively acid backbone; approachable sweetness without Aszú complexity.
budget_wines.tip: The Late Harvest serves well as a dessert wine by the glass for under EUR 15 at Budapest wine bars; it is the best value introduction to Royal Tokaji's house style.

budget_wines.name: Disznókő Tokaji Furmint
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Disznókő cellar door at Tarcal; distributed in Hungary by Bortársaság; available in UK, Germany and the United States via specialist importers.
budget_wines.description: The dry Furmint entry-level from the AXA-owned Disznókő estate. Vibrant lime-and-green-apple freshness with the region's characteristic mineral and taut acid structure. A reliable example of modern Tokaj dry white that represents the region accessibly at under EUR 20.
budget_wines.tip: Chill well before serving; the high natural acidity of Tokaj Furmint needs 30 minutes in an ice bucket to open its citrus aromatics fully.

budget_wines.name: Oremus Mandolás Furmint
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Oremus cellar door at Tolcsva; Bortársaság Budapest; international distribution via Vega Sicilia's importer network.
budget_wines.description: The Mandolás is Oremus's dry Furmint from the Mandolás vineyard plot in Tolcsva, one of the estate's key parcels. The wine carries a floral-herbal lift from the Furmint vine alongside stone-fruit and almond notes that give the wine its name. A consistent budget-tier dry Tokaj offering from a producer of deep heritage.
budget_wines.tip: The Mandolás vineyard sits on the south-facing tuff slopes immediately behind the Oremus cellar; ask for the vineyard explanation during any cellar visit.

budget_wines.name: Patricius Dry Furmint
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Patricius estate at Bodrogkisfalud; distributed across Hungary through Bortársaság and major supermarkets; available in Germany via wine specialist importers.
budget_wines.description: Patricius produces a well-regarded dry Furmint from their Bodrogkisfalud estate, offering fresh citrus and mineral expression with the characteristic Tokaj acid spine. It represents some of the best value in the entire region at under EUR 17, with consistent quality across vintages.
budget_wines.tip: Patricius is one of the larger Hegyalja family estates and offers solid value across its entire range; the dry Furmint is the entry point but the Late Harvest also exceeds its price category.

budget_wines.name: Béres Lőcse Furmint
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Béres Winery cellar door at Erdőbénye; Bortársaság Budapest and major Hungarian wine retail chains.
budget_wines.description: The Béres estate at Erdőbénye focuses on the Furmint grape across both dry and sweet styles. The Lőcse dry Furmint is the estate's accessible entry bottling, with the house's characteristic stone-fruit and zesty citrus profile from the Erdőbénye terroir. Béres is among the most visitor-friendly estates in the eastern Hegyalja villages.
budget_wines.tip: Erdőbénye is a notably scenic village in the Tokaj region; pair the cellar visit with a walk through the baroque village centre.

budget_wines.name: Holdvölgy Notabene Furmint
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Holdvölgy cellar door at Mád; Budapest distribution via Bortársaság and specialist wine bars.
budget_wines.description: Holdvölgy, the Mád estate, produces the Notabene as its entry-level dry Furmint, sourced from younger vineyard plots around Mád. It delivers the terroir's characteristic citrus-mineral tautness at a price accessible to daily-drinking. Holdvölgy is best known for higher-tier single-vineyard Furmints, and the Notabene provides a reliable gateway to the house style.
budget_wines.tip: Holdvölgy's cellar in Mád is open for appointment tastings; a flight from Notabene up through the single-vineyard bottlings is an excellent vertical way to understand Mád's terroir expression.

budget_wines.name: Hétszőlő Dry Furmint
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Hétszőlő cellar door at Tokaj town; available via Japanese-market importers and Hungarian specialist wine retail.
budget_wines.description: Hétszőlő, the historic estate at Tokaj town owned by Suntory since the early 1990s, produces a dry Furmint from its classified first-class vineyard plots. The historic Hétszőlő site, overlooking the confluence of the Tisza and Bodrog, produces Furmint of good mineral depth and precision at an accessible price level.
budget_wines.tip: The Hétszőlő vineyard has history stretching to the 1737 Primae Classis classification; a visit to the cellar explains the estate's position within the 18th-century classification system.

budget_wines.name: Sauska Tokaj Furmint
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Sauska estate at Erdőbénye and Villány; Bortársaság Budapest and other major wine retailers in Hungary.
budget_wines.description: The Sauska family operates in both the Tokaj and Villány wine regions, and their Tokaj Furmint is sourced from their Erdőbénye estate. The dry Furmint carries the estate's characteristic freshness and mineral precision, with good depth for the price tier. Sauska exports to multiple markets and their Tokaj bottlings are widely distributed.
budget_wines.tip: Sauska's dual-region presence (Tokaj and Villány) makes them a convenient bridge for visitors exploring Hungarian wine more broadly; the Budapest restaurant Sauska Borház showcases both regions.

budget_wines.name: Erzsébet Pince Tokaji Hárslevelü
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Erzsébet Pince cellar door in the Tokaj wine region; available in Budapest specialist wine shops.
budget_wines.description: One of the few budget-tier bottles in Tokaj to showcase Hárslevelü as a single-variety dry wine rather than defaulting to Furmint. Hárslevelü delivers a rounder, more floral and linden-blossom aromatic profile compared to the more austere Furmint, offering a distinct stylistic alternative at a comparable price point.
budget_wines.tip: Hárslevelü is often underappreciated relative to Furmint; this bottling is an excellent way to taste the difference between Tokaj's two principal varieties side by side.

budget_wines.name: Grand Tokaj Entry Furmint
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Widely available in Hungarian supermarkets and wine shops; Grand Tokaj is the region's cooperative producer and distributes nationally at accessible price points.
budget_wines.description: Grand Tokaj is the cooperative winery drawing on fruit from multiple Hegyalja producers, and their entry Furmint represents the most accessible price tier in the entire region. Clean, fresh and correctly dry with citrus and mild stone-fruit character; the best option for a first-time Tokaj dry Furmint purchase in a Hungarian supermarket.
budget_wines.tip: Grand Tokaj's volume production means their entry Furmint is the most shelf-stable and consistently available Tokaj wine in Hungarian retail; it illustrates the regional baseline style well.
